summary: SWSERoll.rollAttack() treats canonicalRollAttack() return value partly like a Roll, but canonicalRollAttack() returns an attack result object with a nested Roll.
evidence: enhanced-rolls.js assigns const roll = await canonicalRollAttack(...) then reads roll.swseAttackContext and roll.dice
attacks.js rollAttack() returns attackResult { roll, total, atkBonus, isHit, isCritical, d20, ... }

summary: Damage buttons preserve only actor/weapon/crit/two-handed style data, not the full attack context snapshot.
evidence: holo-roll.hbs .swse-roll-damage dataset contains actorId, weaponId, isCritical, critMultiplier, twoHanded
chat-interaction-bridge.js handleLegacyDamageRollButton passes only isCritical, critMultiplier, twoHanded, target

summary: CombatActionsMapper._normalizeAction() drops fields needed by the routing contract.
evidence: _normalizeAction() returns key, name, actionType, cost, notes, relatedSkills, dc, outcome, when
sheet registerAction() can use resolutionMode, manualResolution, executable, ruleData, requiredContext, spendAction if present, but mapper may not pass them through

summary: Direct weapon attack cards always spend Standard Action and do not know named attack/action context unless added by the dialog.
Apply Damage only passes a number and threshold flag, not a damage packet.
CombatEngine/CombatExecutor contain full orchestration methods, but active sheet attack path appears to bypass them for normal weapon attacks.
evidence: character-sheet.js normal attacks route to SWSERoll.rollAttack()
